missed_sites: internal/hook/hook.go:53-57 — the Names slice still lists "pre-complete" (a registered hook name). The spec's In-Scope enumerates precomplete.go, dispatch.go, setup/claude.go, cmd/mindspec/setup.go, and the two precomplete_*_test.go files, but NOT hook.go's Names slice. After R1 removes the dispatch arm, "pre-complete" is a dangling registered name; if removed from Names then `mindspec hook pre-complete` becomes an `unknown hook` error (cmd/mindspec/hook.go:44), if left it dispatches to default:Pass. Either way Names must be updated and the spec must say so.

internal/harness/scenario_panel_gate.go — the ScenarioPanelGateBlocksPrematureComplete harness scenario (run by TestLLM_PanelGateBlocksPrematureComplete) invokes the LIVE `mindspec hook pre-complete --format=claude` subprocess (L181) and HARD-ASSERTS it Blocks with exit 2 (L194/L198) and emits the incomplete-panel block text (L203-210). After R1 retires the hook, that command either errors (removed from Names) or exits 0/Pass — the scenario's assertions BREAK. This file is not listed in the spec's In-Scope at all. Worse, the spec's own Validation Proofs deliberately AVOID `go test ./internal/harness/...` (R2 ⚠️ constraint), so this break is invisible to the spec's stated gates.
blocking: Add internal/hook/hook.go (the Names slice / --list output, plus TestNames_Sorted in hook_test.go) to In Scope: "pre-complete" must be dropped from Names when the dispatch arm is removed, otherwise it is a dead registered name (and `mindspec hook pre-complete` either errors or silently Passes).

Add internal/harness/scenario_panel_gate.go + its test (scenario_panel_gate_test.go / scenario.go registration) to In Scope. This LLM harness scenario asserts the pre-complete hook Blocks a premature complete; retiring the hook makes those assertions false. The scenario must be removed or repointed at the in-binary `mindspec complete` gate (which IS the authoritative enforcement R1 preserves). Note this crosses the execution-owned internal/harness/** boundary, which the spec currently scopes only to R2's analyzer.go — an Impacted-Domains/ownership note is needed.

Confirm the setup auto-removal claim is exercised: dropping the pre-complete entry from wantedHooks() makes it stale via removeStaleMindspecEntries (keep-list = wanted, ownership by `mindspec hook ` prefix), so existing installs ARE cleaned WITHOUT a new spec-072-style retired list. The spec's In-Scope wording ("mirroring the spec-072 retired-hook precedent") slightly overstates the need — the generic stale-removal already covers it. Not blocking, but the AC should assert removal, e.g. via a test that a pre-existing `mindspec hook pre-complete` entry is gone after setup.
summary: R1 covers the obvious surface (precomplete.go matcher+gate, dispatch.go arm, setup/claude.go + cmd/mindspec/setup.go install, the two precomplete_*_test.go files, ADR-0037 amendment), and the setup auto-removal of a pre-existing install works mechanically via removeStaleMindspecEntries. But two removal sites are MISSED: (1) the "pre-complete" entry in internal/hook/hook.go's Names slice (a dangling registered hook name / --list entry), and (2) internal/harness/scenario_panel_gate.go — a live LLM harness scenario that invokes `mindspec hook pre-complete` and hard-asserts it Blocks (exit 2). Site (2) is a genuine test break the spec does not scope, and it is invisible to the spec's own validation proofs because they avoid `go test ./internal/harness/...`. CHANGES until both sites are enumerated in Scope and the harness scenario is removed or repointed at the in-binary gate.
